title: acanthopsis dregeana subsp. longispina
intro: Acanthopsis dregeana subsp. longispina is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as the long-spined acanthopsis. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues related to fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The leaves and stems of the Acanthopsis dregeana subsp. longispina plant are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which are believed to possess antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, contributing to its health benefits. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused in water to treat fever, and its leaves can be crushed and applied topically to alleviate skin irritations and wounds.
